reviews.review_description: Makes me pay $20 JUST TO FLY. I've always wanted to fly a plane in vr and if you don't want to spend money just to fly, do not get this game. Before i got this game i read the reviews. I thought to myself " pshhhh, 1.5? They are just being dramatic!" well... I was wrong. Overall, just please do not get this game.

I downloaded this but as far as I can see the only thing you can do is run through two tutorials and neither gives you access to any controls in the plane and certainly no way to actually start/fly one. So what's the point of a flight simulator you can't fly?

Surely they can't be expecting you to go ahead pay for access to a plane without even giving you at least a few seconds to see what the interactivity and handling is like?

Don't waste your time with this.

This game has an great tutorial, but absolutely no way to play anything but the tutorial. You have to pay $20 just to be able to get a plane, and you don’t even know if it has good operations. Definitely don’t download this if you don’t have money to pay for the full game￼

I downloaded this game thinking i would would fly but NOPE i get to go through 2 tutorials where a barely did CRAP and now to fly i have to pay $20 dollars to fly pls do not download its not worth your time and to those who did download it sorry for your pain Trent just quit get a job.

Warning: In-app purchase is non-refundable
Be very careful with this game. The in-app purchase is non-refundable, even if you’ve barely played or didn’t realise what you were buying. Meta support confirmed they cannot refund in-app purchases at all. I wish this was made clearer before purchase. Just posting this so others don’t get caught out like I did.
Really enjoying this game, great introduction to flying planes and honestly I can spend a lot of time just flying around and practicing my landing.

reviews.review_title: I should have read the reviews.
reviews.review_description: They trap you into a non-refundable purchase by making it an in-game purchase. This behaves very little like an actual aircraft. There is no training value to this at all. I have over 10,000 hours as a commercial pilot, and I promise this is a waste of time and money. You would do better flushing a $20 bill down the toilet.

developer_response.body:
Hello chris.lykins.10,
The App was developed with flying schools as a tool to assist with learning the ‘how’ of flying - procedures, flows and systems inside a realistic VR cockpit you fly with your hands. The aim was to replace the analog cockpit photos that many students sit in front of for hours learning.  The pricing was changed on 6 January 2026 for the latest release 1.3.78 after reviewing comments over the past year.  Aircraft and Airfields are now a fixed price add-on as requested by many users rather than an hourly price.    There is no requirement to subscribe or pay on an ongoing basis.
Please take a look at the update, support our development through purchasing the Add-ons and update your comments to be correct.
https://www.meta.com/en-gb/experiences/bristell-classic-aircraft-yscb-airport-pack/1201271614799389/

reviews.review_description: at first I thought it was fun and free until I figured out it costs $24!!! for an hour do not try or buy the time I am serious do not download this

I've never used a flight SIM before, so for me it was novel to be sat in a cockpit on a runway surrounded by switches and dials. I intuitively switched on the master switches, turned on the engine, pushed the throttle forward, released the parking brake, and was going down the runway. I pulled back when it felt fast enough and was airborne. I dunno, it was intuitive to me. Crashed almost immediately though. So on my 2nd attempt I touched the blue-tick which opened up a basic "push this, turn that" kind of tutorial which was more helpful. I managed to get airborne and went higher and higher. I thought it was alright. This would suit someone who is brand new to VR (as I am). I didn't mind paying £15.99 for the experience - but I do acknowledge it should be around half that price. I found it relaxing and novel though. My only gripes are the almost total lack of meaningful tutorial in the cockpit. It tells you to flick this switch and that, and assumes you know already what "carb heat" is, for instance. It tells you to move controls without telling you why. It assumes you're already familiar with the basics of flight. A separate tutorial on flight basics would be very handy. However, I managed to kind of pick it up on the fly (see what I did there?). The other gripe is using hands. It was difficult to just turn a dial or pull a knob. It doesn't work too well. I was trying to turn the choke off mid-flight but I kept grabbing the throttle or the propeller lever, and couldn't quite pinch the choke one. My flying was all over the place as I took my concentration off to get control of the choke lever, and never actually achieved it.
We cant even figure out how to get into the plane and fly. Paid for the expansion pack and still can figure it out. Should have read the reviews it is a scam.
WAY too much money to fly and the tutorials you can’t even interact with anything. Make it free or less expensive and maybe the review will change.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your recent purchase of our 1-hour Add-on. We truly appreciate your support and apologise for the timing of our update. The recent update introduced new features and changes to improve user experience and reduced the price of Add-ons to a single purchase, but we understand this may have impacted your session unexpectedly. We’d love the chance to make this right. Please reach out to our support team at https://capitalxr.com.au/contact and provide your username and purchase dates of the Add-ons so we can review your account and discuss options for a suitable resolution.
Your feedback is valuable and helps us improve. 
Thank you for giving us the opportunity to address this.
